intro: Rhynchosia crinita, also known as climbing milk-pea or hairy Rhynchosia, is a flowering plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhynchosia crinita include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Rhynchosia crinita is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in traditional African medicine to treat various ailments.
